de.uka.ilkd.key.util.joinrule

Class SimultaneousJavaASTWalker

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.util.joinrule.SimultaneousJavaASTWalker
  • Direct Known Subclasses:
    SimultaneousJavaASTVisitor

    public abstract class SimultaneousJavaASTWalker
extends Object
    Walks through the Java AST of two program elements simultaneously in depth-left-fist-order at default. Implementing method doAction specifies its behavior at the different Nodes. The depth-left-fist behavior can be changed by overwriting the method walk(ProgramElement).
    Author:
    Dominic Scheurer
    See Also:
    JavaASTWalker

      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      private int depth
      The currently visited level.
      private ProgramElement e1
      The roots where the walker starts.
      private ProgramElement e2
      The roots where the walker starts.
      private boolean incompatible
      True iff the given program elements have a different structure.

      • SimultaneousJavaASTWalker

        public SimultaneousJavaASTWalker(ProgramElement e1,

        Parameters:
        e1 - The first ProgramElement to start with.
        e2 - The second ProgramElement to start with.

    • Method Detail

      • getFirstRoot

        public ProgramElement getFirstRoot()
        Returns the first start point of the walker.
        Returns:
        First root of the AST to walk through.

      • getSecondRoot

        public ProgramElement getSecondRoot()
        Returns the second start point of the walker.
        Returns:
        Second root of the AST to walk through.

      • isIncompatible

        public boolean isIncompatible()
        Returns:
        True iff the given program elements are incompatible (that is, differ in more than just the naming of the program variables).

      • setIncompatible

        public void setIncompatible()
        Parameters:
        incompatible - Call iff the given program elements are incompatible (that is, differ in more than just the naming of the program variables).

      • start

        public void start()
        Starts the walker.

      • depth

        public int depth()
        Returns:
        The currently visited level.

      • walk

        protected void walk(ProgramElement node1,
        ProgramElement node2)
        Walks through the ASTs simultaneously, while keeping track of the current nodes.
        Parameters:
        node1 - The first JavaProgramElement the walker is at.
        node2 - The second JavaProgramElement the walker is at.

      • doAction

        protected abstract void doAction(ProgramElement node1,
            ProgramElement node2)
        The action that is performed just before leaving the node the last time.
